_comment_bootloader: removable:false is load-bearing on a shared ESP -- do not drop it.
lands in EFI/BOOT/BOOTX64.EFI, the removable-media fallback path, and NO
efibootmgr entry is created. On a single-OS disk that still boots, via the
fallback. On a disk that already has Windows it does not: Windows' own
NVRAM entry wins the BootOrder and you reboot straight into Windows,
looking exactly like Arch failed to install. It also overwrites
EFI/BOOT/BOOTX64.EFI, which on many dual-boot ESPs is Windows' fallback
copy. With false, GRUB installs to EFI/GRUB/ and takes the first NVRAM
slot -- which is what docs/arch-install.md describes.
_comment_packages: openssh is here so `systemctl enable sshd` is actually possible before the
greeter reboot -- the README calls that cheap insurance, and it is, but
base does not ship openssh and there is no network to install it from if
